sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.52 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: (N.\,Eggert). Let$R$be a commutative associative finite-dimensional nilpotent algebra over a finite field$F$of characteristic$p$. Let$R^{(p)}$be the subalgebra of all elements of the form$r^p$($r R$). Is it true that$R p\, R^{(p)}$? An affirmative answer gives a solution to 11.44 for the case where the factors$A$and$B$are abelian. { L.\,S.\,Kazarin}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
